summary: The app has a solid TypeScript foundation and clean component architecture, but lacks critical performance optimizations, testing, CI/CD, and monitoring for production readiness.

critical.title: MapView re-renders on every unrelated state change
critical.description: MapView is embedded inside large stateful components with 15+ useState hooks. Any state change (typing, OTP entry, voice) triggers full re-render including MapView, causing jank and dropped frames on mid-range devices.
critical.fix: Extract MapView into a React.memo'd wrapper component. Pass only coordinates and markers as props. Use stable references for marker data.

critical.title: BuyerHomeScreen has 15+ useState hooks causing cascading re-renders
critical.description: The form screen manages title, description, address, search, suggestions, voice, location, budget, urgency, and UI state all as separate useState calls. Any keystroke re-renders the entire component tree including MapView and suggestions list.
critical.fix: Group related state with useReducer or a form library. Isolate the map section into a memoized child component.

critical.title: HelperTaskScreen is 841 lines with nested ScrollView containing MapView
critical.description: Massive monolithic screen with task loading, selfie capture, OTP entry, location tracking, route fetching, rating, cancellation, and celebration modal all in one component. Every state update re-renders everything.
critical.fix: Split into sub-components: MapSection, TaskStatusCard, SelfieCapture, RatingCard, CelebrationModal. Wrap each with React.memo.

critical.title: BuyerHomeScreen sets controlled `region` prop causing map jumpiness
critical.description: The MapView has both `initialRegion` and `region` set. The `region` prop is recalculated from state on every render, overriding user pan/zoom and causing jumpy behavior.
critical.fix: Remove the controlled `region` prop. Use `mapRef.animateToRegion()` for programmatic region changes only when coordinates actually change.

critical.title: Zero test files — no unit, integration, or E2E tests
critical.description: No testing framework configured. No jest.config, no test scripts, no __tests__ directory. This means zero confidence in any refactoring.
critical.fix: Add jest + @testing-library/react-native. Create test scaffold. Add E2E with Detox/Maestro.

critical.title: No CI/CD pipeline
critical.description: No GitHub Actions, no fastlane, no automated builds. Manual deployments are error-prone and don't catch regressions.
critical.fix: Add GitHub Actions workflow for lint, typecheck, and test on PRs. Add build scripts for Android/iOS.

critical.title: No crash reporting or performance monitoring
critical.description: No Sentry, no Firebase Performance, no Bugsnag. Crashes in production will be invisible.
critical.fix: Add @sentry/react-native with error boundaries. Add Firebase Performance for startup and network metrics.

critical.title: Duplicated polyline decoding function — inlined in two files
critical.description: Polyline decoding (~60 lines) is copy-pasted in two screen files. The HelperTaskScreen version is inlined inside a useEffect and re-parses on every render cycle where dependencies change.
critical.fix: Extract to a shared `src/utils/geo.ts` utility. Import in both screens.

critical.title: Duplicated distanceMeters utility function in two files
critical.description: Haversine distance calculation is duplicated across two screen files.

critical.title: SocketProvider creates new socket on every token change without cleanup race guard
critical.description: When accessToken changes (e.g., on refresh), the old socket disconnects and a new one is created. The eslint-disable comment suggests the dependency array was intentionally trimmed, but this can cause orphaned socket references in state if the effect fires rapidly.
critical.fix: Add proper cleanup. Store socket in a ref. Only recreate if auth status actually transitions. Add reconnect/backoff.

critical.title: HistoryScreen FlatList row is not memoized — inline renderItem
critical.description: The renderItem callback creates new JSX inline on every render. No React.memo wrapper on the row component. With many tasks, this causes FlatList to re-render all visible rows on any state change.
critical.fix: Extract a memoized HistoryRow component. Use stable renderItem and keyExtractor.

critical.title: No image caching — using raw <Image> and react-native-image-picker without cache
critical.description: Selfie images are not cached after capture. No react-native-fast-image or expo-image. Screen transitions will reload images from disk or from remote URLs, causing flicker.
critical.fix: Use expo-image (Expo-compatible FastImage alternative) for displaying cached images. Add preview immediately after capture.

critical.title: No skeleton/placeholder loaders — full-screen ActivityIndicator used
critical.description: Loading states show bare text or nothing. No content placeholders. This creates perceived jank during data loading.
critical.fix: Add SkeletonPlaceholder components for task cards, map areas, and list items.

critical.title: No Reanimated — celebration modal/acceptance uses no native animation
critical.description: The celebration/completion modal appears with no animation. No marker pop animations. No native-driven transitions. This feels jarring on task state changes.
critical.fix: Add react-native-reanimated. Use entering/exiting animations for modals. AnimatedRegion for markers.

critical.title: Google Places API called directly from client — no debounce on autocomplete
critical.description: Place autocomplete fires on a 350ms setTimeout per keystroke without proper debounce. Rapid typing creates multiple inflight requests that race. This also exposes the API key in client-side requests.
critical.fix: Use proper debounce (lodash.debounce or AbortController). Consider proxying through backend to hide API key.

medium.title: No global state management — entirely Context-based with prop drilling
medium.description: State is spread across three Context providers. Any context value change re-renders all consumers. No selector pattern to prevent unneeded renders.
medium.fix: Migrate to Zustand or Redux Toolkit with selective subscriptions. Keep Context for simple auth state.

medium.title: Socket events not debounced — rapid task_status_changed events cause cascading renders
medium.description: Socket event handlers call setTask/setHelperLoc immediately. Burst events (e.g., helper moving fast) will trigger multiple state updates in quick succession.
medium.fix: Batch socket updates using requestAnimationFrame or a short debounce. Use functional state updates consistently.

medium.title: BuyerTaskScreen Direction API route fetch fires on every helperLoc change
medium.description: The useEffect for route fetching depends on `helperLoc` and `task`. While there is a 12s throttle, the dependency array means the effect recreates on every location update, creating unnecessary work.
medium.fix: Move the route fetch interval into a separate ref-based approach. Only fire when helperLoc changes significantly (>50m).

medium.title: helperLastSeen memo uses Date.now() but never updates — stale data
medium.description: The useMemo for `helperLastSeen` calls Date.now() only when `helperLoc` changes. Between location updates, the displayed 'updated X seconds ago' value is frozen.
medium.fix: Use a setInterval to update the 'last seen' timer every few seconds, or display the absolute time.

medium.title: Google Maps API key exposed in client-side fetch URLs
medium.description: The GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is sent directly in client-side fetch URLs to googleapis.com. While Android/iOS keys can be restricted by bundle ID, the key is still extractable from network traffic.
medium.fix: Restrict the key by bundle identifier in Google Cloud Console. Consider proxying through your backend for Places/Directions.

medium.title: BuyerTaskScreen is not split — 577 lines with map + status + rating + cancel all in one
medium.description: Similar to HelperTaskScreen: massive monolithic component, but slightly smaller. Still causes unnecessary re-renders of map when typing in cancel/rating fields.
medium.fix: Extract MapSection, StatusCard, RatingCard, CancelSection into memoized sub-components.

medium.title: Double load() calls — useEffect + useFocusEffect both fire on mount
medium.description: Both useEffect and useFocusEffect call load() on mount, resulting in duplicate API requests on first render.
medium.fix: Remove the useEffect load() call and rely only on useFocusEffect, which fires on both mount and re-focus.

medium.title: No ESLint config — only basic TypeScript checking
medium.description: No .eslintrc, no prettier config. Code style is maintained manually. No automated linting in dev workflow or CI.
medium.fix: Add @typescript-eslint, eslint-config-@react-native, prettier, and lint scripts.

medium.title: No InteractionManager usage for deferring non-critical work
medium.description: Heavy operations like location resolution, autocomplete fetching, and storage I/O run immediately without deferring via InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions.
medium.fix: Wrap non-critical operations (analytics, storage persistence, address resolution) with InteractionManager.

medium.title: No offline retry/queue for selfie uploads — error shown and lost
medium.description: If selfie upload fails, the captured image is discarded. The user must re-capture. No retry queue or offline support.
medium.fix: Cache the captured image and implement a retry queue. Show the cached preview immediately while uploading in background.

prs.title: PR1: Memoization, FlatList optimizations, and skeleton loaders
prs.summary: Wrap heavy screen sections with React.memo, extract memoized row components, add skeleton placeholders during loading, fix double API calls on mount.
acceptance: FlatList rows re-render < 2x on store update
No double API call on mount

prs.title: PR2: Map performance — memoized wrapper, throttled region, animated markers
prs.summary: Extract MapView into MemoizedMapView wrapper, throttle region changes, remove controlled region prop, use AnimatedRegion for helper marker, extract shared geo utilities.
acceptance: Map does not re-render on text input

prs.title: PR3: Image caching, compression, and upload queue
prs.summary: Add expo-image for cached display, compress selfies before upload, implement background upload queue with retry.
acceptance: Selfie preview shows immediately after capture
expo-image displays cached images without flicker

prs.title: PR4: Reanimated v2 rewrite for modals and transitions
prs.summary: Replace static celebration modal with animated entrance/exit via Reanimated. Add marker pop animation on task acceptance.
tests: Jest: CelebrationModal renders with entering animation prop

prs.title: PR5: TypeScript strictness, ESLint, Sentry, CI, and dev tooling
prs.summary: Strengthen tsconfig, add ESLint + Prettier, integrate Sentry error reporting, create GitHub Actions CI workflow, add PR template.

rolloutPlan.week3: PR5 (TypeScript strictness, Sentry, CI, E2E scaffolding)
rolloutPlan.week4: Release canary via TestFlight/Internal Track, collect metrics, fix regressions, then production release
